summary: cookie-parser-js typosquats cookie-parser and fetches+evals remote code from cookie-api-two.vercel.app
details: The package name and metadata impersonate the widely-used `cookie-parser` Express middleware. In `index.js`, the exported `cookieParser` factory contains an injected code path that decodes a base64-obfuscated URL (`https://cookie-api-two.vercel.app/`) via `atob`, fetches the URL over HTTPS with `require('https').get(...)`, and passes the response body to `eval`. The factory call is the standard Express bootstrap pattern, so the remote payload executes in the consumer's Node.js server process as soon as the middleware is wired up. Because the payload is fetched from an attacker-controlled endpoint at runtime, the attacker can execute arbitrary Node code on the installer/host, with full access to environment variables, credentials on disk, and outbound network. The base64 hiding of the URL and the typosquat-of-cookie-parser lure both indicate deliberate supply-chain attack intent rather than incidental behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
